description: An explicit solver blows up to infinity because the step size exceeded its stability region for a stiff system.
body: Failure mode: an explicit method like forward Euler runs at a step larger than its stability limit on a stiff system, so per-step error amplifies until the solution diverges. Detection signals: values growing exponentially toward infinity or NaN, divergence that worsens with a larger step, and stability returning only at an impractically tiny step. Do not keep shrinking the step indefinitely for a genuinely stiff system. Safer fallback: switch to an implicit or adaptive solver whose stability region tolerates the stiffness, then confirm a bounded solution at a usable step.
